description: Shared correctness fixtures (docs/benchmark-correctness-fixtures.md). Both languages implement the identical four kernels with 64-bit wrapping semantics (Java long overflow == Rust wrapping_*): xorshift64 scalar mix, xorshift64-filled array reduction, decimal CSV parser over a deterministically generated input, and an LCG stateful counter. Every value below must be produced exactly by BOTH suites — trap variants and corrected variants alike compute the same result (only the measurement harness differs). Change a value here only together with both test files.

semantics.xorshift64: x ^= x << 13; x ^= x >>> 7; x ^= x << 17 (64-bit wrapping, logical right shift)
semantics.reduction: wrapping 64-bit sum over the xorshift64 stream (each element is the stream value AFTER stepping)
semantics.parser: input = comma-joined decimal of (stream value mod 1000000), count values from the seed; checksum = checksum*31 + parsedValue, 64-bit wrapping; the parse must also verify the value count
semantics.counter: state = state * 6364136223846793005 + 1442695040888963407 (64-bit wrapping LCG), stepped N times from the seed
